old_claim.note: Astrolabe & Quadrant provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Compass and Astrolabe & Quadrant; Navigational magnetic compasses do not require astrolabes or quadrants; they solve direction finding through magnetic alignment rather than astronomical altitude measurement.

source_shape.source_locator: History section: mariners in China and Europe discovered lodestone/needle direction-finding for navigation in the 12th century.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Navigational magnetic compasses do not require astrolabes or quadrants; they solve direction finding through magnetic alignment rather than astronomical altitude measurement.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Compass claim or adjacent chronology without requiring Astrolabe & Quadrant as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Compass treated Astrolabe & Quadrant as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Compass no longer directly depends on Astrolabe & Quadrant; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: compass must not directly depend on astrolabe_quadrant unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows compass is intentionally scoped only to cases that require astrolabe_quadrant.
The graph splits compass and reserves this id for a narrower technology that directly depends on astrolabe_quadrant.
A stronger source demonstrates that astrolabe_quadrant is chronologically and causally necessary for compass.
short_reason: Navigational magnetic compasses do not require astrolabes or quadrants; they solve direction finding through magnetic alignment rather than astronomical altitude measurement.
